channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: With this simple gadget, you can make great-tasting pour-over coffee with fewer beans
item.description: Brewing coffee is all about extraction, and the right kettle will help enormously. Here's how to choose one.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI Updates: White House Implementing AI in Federal Records, 'Wizard of Oz' Transformation, OpenAI vs. Musk
item.description: Get the latest on AI technology advancements, challenges, and opportunities for future development with updates from Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York AG urges Congress to strengthen crypto regulations after DOJ disbands task force
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James (D) sent a Thursday letter to leaders in Congress urging them to crack down on crypto regulations days after the Justice Department (DOJ) disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Social Security Administration 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Venmo’s dubious defaults look like a permanent privacy foul
item.description: If it weren’t for Signal, Venmo might be the most infamous app of the Trump administration—and maybe the most beloved among journalists covering this White House.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: This tiny screw is powering the humanoid robot revolution
item.description: The humanoid robotics revolution is just around the corner. Test models are already working in factories alongside human beings across the world, while AI companies develop new foundation models designed to help robots navigate their environments as easily as humans do.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Why I read 597 applications for one job—no AI involved
item.description: Last month I posted a job description on our blog for a chief of staff role at my venture capital firm, Graham & Walker. Turns out, that job description really hit a nerve.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Feinzaig, Fast Company

item.title: Xenotransplant Disappointment: Alabama Woman's Pig Kidney Removed After 130 Days
item.description: Alabama woman Towana Looney had a pig kidney removed after 130 days, marking a disappointment in the quest for animal-to-human transplants. Scientists are genetically altering pigs to create human-like organs.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s revenge politics has a new target: the cybersecurity official who defied him in 2020
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday ordered federal officials to investigate the former head of the country’s top cybersecurity agency and revoke his security clearances, years after he first disputed Trump’s election fraud claims.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE just announced more layoffs at TTS, the government’s vaunted tech group, sources say
item.description: Thomas Shedd, who directs the General Services Administration’s vaunted tech group, Technology Transformation Services (TTS), has just announced that a new round of firings has begun at TTS, insiders tells Fast Company.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Commission greenlights Microsoft’s data center plans in North San Jose
item.description: Microsoft is moving forward with a data center project in North San Jose as the tech giant pauses other investment plans around the country
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again
item.description: If DOGE is looking for a high-value, high-return target, the software procurement process is an easy win. The post A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: These tech leaders donated to Donald Trump. Now they’re out billions of dollars
item.description: While Elon Musk may be in the spotlight due to his proximity to Trump, he’s not the xonly prominent tech figure to experience recent losses.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple has few incentives to start making iPhones in U.S., despite Trump’s trade war with China
item.description: It would take several years and cost billions of dollars to build new plants in the U.S., and then confront Apple with economic forces that could triple the price of an iPhone, threatening to torpedo sales of its marquee product.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s AI policy shifts focus to ‘high impact’ use cases
item.description: The White House says a new federal AI policy rejects the Biden administration's “risk-averse” approach, but one former official sees it as a “smart evolution." The post Trump’s AI policy shifts focus to ‘high impact’ use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Android feature prevents sensitive content in Google Messages
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how Google kept Android SafetyCore details quiet until it scanned users' photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China’s rare earth export controls threaten enterprise IT hardware supply chains
item.description: China has announced immediate export controls on seven more rare earth elements critical to enterprise IT hardware manufacturing, firing a fresh salvo in the ongoing tech trade war.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
